name: Listening assessment bank
note: Prototype (path A): each item carries a 'script' spoken via the browser's TTS; the transcript is NOT shown. Real version pre-generates audio files (path B). AI-authored draft.

questions.script: Man: What time does the train to Boston leave? Woman: It leaves at seven forty-five from platform three.
questions.question: What time does the train leave?
questions.explanation: The woman says it leaves at seven forty-five.

questions.script: Welcome to the museum. The café is on the second floor and closes at six p.m.
questions.question: Where is the café?
questions.explanation: The announcement says the café is on the second floor.

questions.script: Woman: I'll have a coffee and a sandwich, please. Man: Sure, that will be five dollars.
questions.question: How much does it cost?
questions.explanation: The man says it will be five dollars.

questions.script: Today I want to talk about how bees help farmers. By moving pollen between flowers, bees make it possible for many crops to grow.
questions.question: What is the talk mainly about?
choices: How to keep bees as pets
questions.explanation: The talk is about bees helping crops via pollination.

questions.script: Man: I've decided to start cycling to work. It's cheaper than driving, and I get some exercise too.
questions.question: Why is the man going to cycle to work?
choices: To save money and get exercise
questions.explanation: He mentions it's cheaper and gives exercise.

questions.script: In this lecture we'll look at remote work. While it gives people flexibility, it can also blur the line between work and home life.
questions.question: What is the lecturer's main point?
choices: Remote work has benefits and drawbacks
Remote work is the best option
questions.explanation: The lecturer notes flexibility but also a downside — both sides.

questions.script: Woman: Are you coming to the picnic on Saturday? Man: I'd love to, but I have to finish my report by Monday.
questions.question: What can we infer about the man?
choices: He probably won't come to the picnic
questions.explanation: Having to finish the report implies he likely won't come.

questions.script: Man: How was the film? Woman: Well... the special effects were nice.
questions.question: What does the woman imply?
choices: She didn't really enjoy the film overall
questions.explanation: Praising only the effects, with hesitation, implies overall disappointment.

questions.script: Professor: Most of you did well on the essay, but several of you clearly wrote it the night before.
questions.question: What is the professor implying?
questions.explanation: 'Wrote it the night before' implies inadequate preparation.
